text: Who is the specific user this app is for?
subtext: e.g., 'Freelance designers who juggle 3–10 clients at once.'
why: A specific target user sharpens the competitor and market-size research in your report.

text: What does your app do that existing tools don't?
why: What sets your app apart from existing tools is central to whether your report finds a real opening in the market.

options: One-off purchase per report / item / project
why: How users pay changes your revenue model, churn risk and what pricing benchmarks we compare you against.

option_notes.Monthly subscription: We'll benchmark monthly SaaS pricing and factor in ongoing churn as a key risk.
option_notes.Annual subscription: We'll benchmark annual pricing and the discount typically needed to get people to commit upfront.
option_notes.One-time license: We'll model a one-off payment per customer, with no recurring revenue to fall back on.
option_notes.Freemium + paid tier: We'll look at typical free-to-paid conversion rates, since most users may never pay.
option_notes.Free with ads: We'll focus on the user volume you'd need for ad revenue to be worthwhile.
option_notes.Per-seat (team plans): We'll benchmark per-seat pricing and factor in how you'll sell into teams, not just individuals.
option_notes.Usage-based / metered: We'll look at typical usage-based pricing and how revenue scales with how much people use it.
option_notes.One-off purchase per report / item / pro: We'll model per-transaction revenue and what volume you'd need to add up.
option_notes.Commission / % per transaction: We'll benchmark typical commission rates and the transaction volume you'd need.

text: If you have a free tier, what's included vs paid?
subtext: Skip if you're not doing free.
why: What's free versus paid shapes how we model your conversion funnel and revenue.

text: Roughly what price do you have in mind, in your local currency?
subtext: Per month if subscription, per item/report if one-off, per seat if team pricing — whichever fits the payment model you picked.

in: One-off purchase per report / item / project

why: Your target price tells us if you're pricing in line with similar tools. Example figures here are just examples — your report prices everything in your own currency, using costs for your country.
Who's building it changes your build costs, timeline and technical risk.

option_notes.I code it myself: We'll assume low cash build costs but factor in your own time as the main constraint.
option_notes.I have a technical co-founder: We'll assume low cash build costs, with two people's time as the main constraint.
option_notes.No-code tools: We'll factor in platform subscription costs and the limits no-code tools put on customisation.
option_notes.I'll hire / outsource development: We'll factor in developer costs and the time it takes to brief and manage outsourced work.

why: Where your users are changes how big a market we size and what compliance or localisation work you'll need.

option_notes.My local city: We'll keep the market sizing tight and focus on local discovery and word of mouth.
option_notes.My country: We'll size the market nationally and keep compliance and localisation to a single country's rules.
option_notes.English-speaking countries: We'll widen the market sizing and flag basic localisation needs across those countries.
option_notes.Global: We'll size the market broadly and flag the localisation, payments and compliance complexity of going worldwide.
